mappings.rationaleAI: Biological inheritance from ancient conceptions through Mendelian genetics addresses early speculations (A.1), Mendel's experiments (A.2), heredity-environment interaction (A.3), chromosomes and genes (B.1), and selective breeding (C.3).

DNA structure, sex linkage, and mutations address Mendelian genetics (A.2), heredity-environment interaction (A.3), chromosomes (B.1), molecular genetics (B.2), population genetics (C.1), and natural selection (C.2).
DNA sequencing and genome assembly address molecular genetics (B.2), and genomic analysis relates to population gene pools (C.1).
DNA structure discovery and Mendel's contributions address Mendelian genetics (A.2) and molecular genetics (B.2).
Natural selection, genetic drift, and speciation address heredity and evolution (C), population genetics (C.1), and natural selection (C.2).
Cell division processes address the cellular basis of heredity (B.1).
Natural selection and the Modern Synthesis address natural selection as an evolutionary agent (C.2).
Eugenic policies address artificial selection for genetic improvements of humankind (C.3).
Plant breeding and livestock management address artificial selection of domesticated organisms (C.3).
Genetic engineering addresses applications of molecular genetics (B.2).
The nature-nurture problem addresses heredity-environment interaction (A.3) and natural selection (C.2).
Genetic manipulation addresses molecular genetics (B.2), and microbial evolution relates to gene frequency changes (C.1).
